channel.title: Insights Aggregator
channel.description: Investment insights from banks & asset managers, aggregated.

item.title: Signals & Noise: U.S. Equity Mid-Year Outlook 2026 — Buy Stocks, Not the Index
item.dc:creator: Bank of America
item.description: Bank of America · Global Research Unlocked — Bank of America's head of U.S. equity strategy argues that investors should focus on individual stock selection rather than broad index exposure in the second half of 2026. With earnings exceeding expectations and inflation pressures potentially triggering Fed rate hikes, she expects large-cap value and manufacturing-related sectors—including energy, materials, industrials, financials, and real estate—to outperform, while mega-cap AI stocks may face headwinds.

item.title: How Will AI Affect Jobs?
item.dc:creator: Goldman Sachs
item.description: Goldman Sachs · Goldman Sachs Exchanges — Experts from MIT and Goldman Sachs examine whether rapid AI improvements and corporate adoption will lead to significant job losses within the decade. The discussion evaluates the validity of concerns around large-scale AI-driven employment displacement and explores the implications outlined in Goldman Sachs' Top of Mind report.

item.title: Mid-year outlook: Prospects remain upbeat for equity and fixed income investors – EN
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · BNPP AM Viewpoint (web) — A mid-year 2026 outlook from BNP Paribas Asset Management highlights optimistic prospects for equity and fixed income investors. The analysis identifies two dominant macroeconomic themes: Middle East geopolitical tensions affecting energy prices and sustained artificial intelligence-driven spending momentum.

item.title: Global Data Pod: W. European Economics
item.dc:creator: JPMorgan
item.description: JPMorgan · Global Data Pod — Greg Fuzesi and Allan Monks discuss the Western European economic outlook in this July 2026 podcast episode. The conversation covers macroeconomic conditions and trends affecting the region's performance and policy environment.

item.title: Global Data Pod: Inflation monitor: Energy relief in sight, but core stays sticky
item.dc:creator: JPMorgan
item.description: JPMorgan · Global Data Pod — Global inflation ticked up in May due to energy prices, but headline inflation is expected to decelerate in the second half of 2026 as oil prices have declined sharply. However, core inflation remains sticky at around 3%, with services price pressures persisting and creating challenges for central bank policy.

item.title: China: Late to the AI sprint, strong in the marathon – EN
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · BNPP AM Viewpoint (web) — China faces significant constraints in cutting-edge AI development due to semiconductor export controls and lacks leading-edge chip capabilities, but may possess structural advantages for long-term AI adoption and deployment across its economy. While the US dominates frontier AI models and chip design, and Taiwan and South Korea control advanced semiconductor manufacturing, China's narrative as a laggard may underestimate its potential in the broader AI marathon beyond just frontier model development.

item.title: Balancing act: Investing amid the AI boom and the Iran war
item.dc:creator: Capital Group
item.description: Capital Group · Capital Ideas — Portfolio manager Paul Benjamin discusses strategies for maintaining balanced investments amid multiple market headwinds including geopolitical tensions in Iran, the artificial intelligence boom, rising inflation, and softening consumer demand. The episode explores how investors can navigate volatility stemming from these diverse sources while preserving portfolio balance.

item.title: Geopolitics and the economy: How war-triggered oil price shock was largely avoided
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · Talking Heads — Despite initial concerns about severe economic impacts from Iran-related conflict, oil prices have remained largely stable due to multiple mitigating factors. The discussion explores how geopolitical tensions and energy market dynamics have evolved differently than predicted, with structural changes in energy markets limiting price shock transmission to the broader economy.

item.title: Monthly Market Views: Political transitions and market caution – EN
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · BNPP AM Viewpoint (web) — BNP Paribas Asset Management leadership discusses how political transitions, particularly UK leadership changes, may create shifts in economic policy and affect market dynamics. The commentary examines implications of political uncertainty for investors across different markets and asset classes.

item.title: El Niño on the horizon
item.dc:creator: HSBC
item.description: HSBC · Under the Banyan Tree — HSBC analysts examine how El Niño weather patterns could broadly impact Asian economies through multiple channels including food price inflation, export restrictions, and energy supply constraints. The discussion explores the cascading economic effects of extreme weather on the region's growth and commodity markets.

item.title: Opportunities in the Space Economy
item.dc:creator: Morgan Stanley
item.description: Morgan Stanley · MS Insights (web) — The space economy is expanding due to increased government funding, private capital inflows, and growing demand for satellite services across multiple applications. Multiple industries across the value chain are positioned to benefit from this growth trend. This represents an emerging investment opportunity in the broader technological and infrastructure sectors.

item.title: 266: 2026 Midyear Outlook: Scarcity vs. Abundance in the Age of AI
item.dc:creator: BlackRock
item.description: BlackRock · The Bid — BlackRock's 2026 midyear outlook examines how AI expansion is creating both opportunities and constraints across energy, infrastructure, labor, and capital. The investment landscape is characterized by multiple competing outcomes rather than a single base case, with geopolitics and structural economic changes reshaping traditional market dynamics and diversification strategies.

item.title: Does AI Raise or Lower Neutral Rates?
item.dc:creator: PIMCO
item.description: PIMCO · PIMCO Pod — PIMCO explores whether artificial intelligence could lower neutral interest rates over time rather than raise them, and examines the implications for monetary policy, bond markets, and economic growth. The discussion considers how productivity gains and deflationary pressures from AI might affect the long-term equilibrium interest rate.

item.title: Research Affiliates
item.dc:creator: Research Affiliates
item.description: Research Affiliates · Research Affiliates Insights — Research Affiliates is a global investment manager that specializes in smart beta strategies, factor-based investing approaches, and systematic asset allocation solutions. The firm provides research-driven investment solutions designed to enhance returns and manage risk across multiple asset classes.

item.title: The Daily Spark
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — The Daily Spark is Apollo's daily data-driven analysis covering US economic conditions, inflation trends, and capital markets performance. Hosted by Chief Economist Torsten Slok, the publication provides economic insights and market outlook updates to keep investors informed of current developments.

item.title: The Daily Spark
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — The Daily Spark is Apollo's daily analysis covering US economic conditions, inflation trends, and capital markets performance. Led by Apollo's Chief Economist, it provides data-driven insights intended to help investors stay informed about market-moving factors and macroeconomic developments.

item.title: What to Watch Ahead of the Midterms
item.dc:creator: Morgan Stanley
item.description: Morgan Stanley · Thoughts on the Market — Morgan Stanley researchers examine consumer trends and sentiment ahead of midterm elections, focusing on what survey data reveals about how voters perceive the economic outlook. With consumer concerns centered on prices and economic conditions, the discussion explores how these trends could influence electoral outcomes in November.

item.title: Inside the AI Value Chain: Reflections from the Road
item.dc:creator: Goldman Sachs
item.description: Goldman Sachs · GSAM Insights (web) — Goldman Sachs Asset Management met with leading technology executives on the US West Coast to gather insights on the current state and evolution of the artificial intelligence landscape. The discussions reflect firsthand perspectives on how AI development and deployment are progressing across the tech industry.

item.title: Investment Views from Our CIOs
item.dc:creator: Goldman Sachs
item.description: Goldman Sachs · GSAM Insights (web) — Goldman Sachs Asset Management's Chief Investment Officers discuss their outlook for the third quarter of 2026, highlighting artificial intelligence, global growth dynamics, and expected returns as primary focus areas. The piece details their current macro perspective and shifts in strategic positioning across their convictions.

item.title: Inflation Climbs Again: The Case for Portfolio Resilience
item.dc:creator: Citi
item.description: Citi · Citi Wealth Market Insights (web) — U.S. inflation has risen to its highest level since April 2023, shifting market expectations toward Fed rate hikes rather than anticipated cuts. Citi Wealth recommends that investors adjust their positioning to account for a sustained higher rate environment rather than the near-term rate reduction scenario that was expected just months ago.

item.title: EM Fixed Income: Seeking guidance into the second half
item.dc:creator: JPMorgan
item.description: JPMorgan · At Any Rate — JPMorgan researchers analyze emerging markets fixed income dynamics and market developments as of mid-2026, offering perspectives on navigating the second half of the year. The discussion covers recent trends impacting the EM fixed income asset class and their implications for investors.

item.title: Cross Asset Research – Societe Generale
item.dc:creator: Société Générale
item.description: Société Générale · SG Cross Asset Research (web) — Société Générale's Cross Asset Research division provides integrated analysis across multiple asset classes including equities, fixed income, currencies, and commodities. The research synthesizes macro and market insights to help investors understand correlations and relationships between different markets and investment categories.

item.title: Seizing opportunity across power, utilities and infrastructure
item.dc:creator: RBC Capital Markets
item.description: RBC Capital Markets · Strategic Alternatives — Global power demand is rising significantly amid geopolitical tensions, driving investment in secure and diversified energy systems. The conversation explores how utilities, midstream operators, and infrastructure investors are adapting to energy transition, digital demand growth from hyperscalers, and climate resilience challenges. Key themes include affordability pressures, an 'all of the above' energy strategy, and the growing importance of energy security to economies.

item.title: Where are markets headed in the second half of 2026?
item.dc:creator: JPMorgan
item.description: JPMorgan · AM · Market Insights — JPMorgan Asset Management provides market predictions for the second half of 2026, analyzing potential AI market corrections, stagflation risks, and economic growth prospects. The outlook includes guidance on asset allocation strategies for investors prioritizing stability and income generation in the near term.

item.title: Perspectives: The Ares story
item.dc:creator: HSBC
item.description: HSBC · HSBC Global Viewpoint — Michael Arougheti, CEO of Ares, discusses the company's origins, expansion drivers, and future growth opportunities in an interview with HSBC's Global Head of Institutional Client Group. The conversation explores Ares' strategic positioning within the alternative asset management industry and its development trajectory.

item.title: Food Security
item.dc:creator: Barclays
item.description: Barclays · Barclays IB Insights & Outlook (web) — Barclays Research examines how geopolitical tensions in Ukraine combined with increasing extreme weather events are creating significant stress on global food supply chains that were already weakened by pandemic-related disruptions. The report analyzes the interconnected risks threatening food security across multiple regions and supply networks.

item.title: Allocation Insights: Asset-Backed Finance Is the Engine Behind Private Credit’s Next Chapter
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — Apollo discusses asset-backed finance as a rapidly expanding segment within private credit markets, examining how structured credit strategies are evolving to reshape portfolio allocations. Michael Paniwozik, Global Head of Structured Credit Investing, explores how asset-backed finance mechanisms are driving the next phase of private credit growth and investor deployment.

item.title: Capital Market Outlook
item.dc:creator: Bank of America
item.description: Bank of America · BofA Private Bank Insights (web) — Bank of America's Private Bank provides a comprehensive capital market outlook as of June 29, 2026, analyzing current conditions and forward-looking perspectives across major asset classes and market segments. The outlook covers economic trends, valuations, and investment positioning relevant to portfolio construction and tactical allocation decisions.

item.title: Mick Mulvaney: Policy, Polarization and the Trump Legacy
item.dc:creator: State Street
item.description: State Street · Street Signals — Mick Mulvaney, former OMB Director and White House Chief of Staff under Trump, discusses the current policy dynamics in Washington, geopolitical considerations shaping the 2nd Trump Administration, and the political legacy that will remain after Trump's term concludes in 2027.

item.title: Re-Mapping the Opportunity in Asia Credit
item.dc:creator: KKR
item.description: KKR · KKR Insights & Macro (web) — Asia credit markets have largely been analyzed using frameworks and assumptions that are now outdated after significant structural changes over the past decade. KKR examines how evolving economic conditions, regulatory environments, and market dynamics require investors to reassess their approach to credit opportunities in the region. Updated mapping of these opportunities can help identify areas of relative value and risk that traditional perspectives may miss.

item.title: Macro Signposts | Does AI Raise or Lower Neutral Rates?
item.dc:creator: PIMCO
item.description: PIMCO · PIMCO Insights & Outlooks (web) — PIMCO economists examine whether artificial intelligence increases or decreases neutral interest rates, analyzing the economic implications of AI productivity gains and their potential impact on long-term rate equilibrium. The analysis considers how AI adoption may alter the structural factors that determine where interest rates settle in the absence of cyclical pressures.

item.title: IPO readiness through a public and private market lens
item.dc:creator: Wellington
item.description: Wellington · Wellington Insights & Outlooks (web) — This discussion examines how public market investors assess companies preparing for initial public offerings, considering factors such as valuation metrics, strategic fit within portfolios, quality of disclosure practices, and investor education initiatives. The analysis bridges perspectives from both private and public market participants to understand what makes IPO candidates attractive to institutional shareholders. Key considerations include management alignment with long-term shareholder interests and the transition from private to public company operations.

item.title: The Market Shift Investors May Be Missing
item.dc:creator: Morgan Stanley
item.description: Morgan Stanley · Thoughts on the Market — Morgan Stanley's CIO Mike Wilson highlights that equity market leadership is expanding beyond concentrated sectors to a broader range of companies, driven by stronger economic and earnings outlooks than consensus expected. Investors who recognize and act on this market shift quickly may capture outsized returns before it becomes widely appreciated.

item.title: Women and wealth
item.dc:creator: Barclays
item.description: Barclays · Barclays Private Bank Insights (web) — Women are increasingly involved in wealth creation and management across global markets, yet often enter financial decision-making only when responsibility transfers to them. This suggests a gap between women's growing economic role and their earlier integration into wealth stewardship and planning. The trend highlights evolving dynamics in how families and institutions approach wealth management and succession planning.

item.title: Don’t Judge an Asset by Its Label
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — This episode examines how insurance companies classify affiliated and related party assets on their balance sheets, explaining that these labels primarily describe the originator of the asset rather than indicating lower quality. The hosts clarify common misconceptions about what the affiliated designation means and its actual implications for asset evaluation.

item.title: Increasing Transparency and Tradability in Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — Private credit markets are evolving toward greater transparency and tradability, following a historical pattern seen in other major credit markets. As these markets mature and scale, asset pricing becomes more frequent, information becomes more accessible, and participation broadens beyond original originators.

item.title: Increasing Transparency and Tradability in Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — Private credit markets are evolving toward greater transparency and tradability, following the historical pattern seen in other major credit markets as they mature and scale. The shift from fragmented information and infrequent pricing to more standardized and liquid trading mechanisms increases accessibility and efficiency in the private credit space.

item.title: Multi-Asset Investments Views: Midnight oil – EN
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · BNPP AM Viewpoint (web) — SpaceX's IPO valued the company at approximately $1.8 trillion, placing it among the world's top 10 firms by market capitalization. This offering represents a significant milestone for public equity markets and the artificial intelligence ecosystem as a major AI-adjacent company entering public markets. The development has implications for multi-asset portfolio management and valuation dynamics across various investment categories.

item.title: The sovereignty challenge: Investment opportunities in a fragmented world – EN
item.dc:creator: BNP Paribas
item.description: BNP Paribas · BNPP AM Viewpoint (web) — As geopolitical tensions rise and global cooperation fragments, the traditional investment framework based on multilateral cooperation is being reshaped. The article examines how economic fragmentation and technological transformation are creating new investment opportunities and challenges in this reconfigured landscape.

item.title: PERSPECTIVES Special - The state of our union
item.dc:creator: Deutsche Bank
item.description: Deutsche Bank · Deutsche Bank Wealth CIO Insights (web) — Deutsche Bank's Wealth CIO examines the structural foundations of US economic resilience as the nation undergoes a transition toward an AI-driven economy. The analysis addresses how innovation, capital allocation, and policy will shape future growth while considering emerging pressures from technology, fiscal dynamics, and global complexity. The piece contextualizes this transformation within the US's 250-year history of reinvention.

item.title: Cross Asset Research – Societe Generale
item.dc:creator: Société Générale
item.description: Société Générale · SG Cross Asset Research (web) — Société Générale's Cross Asset Research division provides integrated analysis across multiple financial markets and asset classes. The research covers insights, publications, and video content designed to help investors understand interconnections between equities, fixed income, commodities, and other markets.

item.title: Cross Asset Research – Societe Generale
item.dc:creator: Société Générale
item.description: Société Générale · SG Cross Asset Research (web) — Société Générale's Cross Asset Research provides analysis spanning multiple asset classes and market insights. The platform aggregates research publications, videos, and strategic perspectives on global financial markets and economic trends.

item.title: Barclays Brief podcast - Episode 38
item.dc:creator: Barclays
item.description: Barclays · Barclays IB Insights & Outlook (web) — A decade after the Brexit referendum, the UK faces shifting political leadership and continued economic adjustments. The episode examines the impact of recent political changes and structural economic considerations affecting the UK market landscape.

item.title: Brexit, Burnham and Britain's Next Decade
item.dc:creator: Barclays
item.description: Barclays · Barclays Brief — Ten years after Brexit, the UK economy faces headwinds including weaker growth, lower investment, and fiscal constraints. The focus is shifting toward forward-looking policy solutions, with devolution to cities and regions emerging as a potential growth driver under new economic leadership. The episode examines current conditions including subdued growth and elevated inflation alongside the UK's underlying economic fundamentals.

item.title: Absolute return strategies & trouble in private credit?
item.dc:creator: JPMorgan
item.description: JPMorgan · On Investors' Minds — With inflation expectations rising and stock-bond correlations increasing, absolute return strategies are gaining attention as portfolio diversifiers. JPMorgan discusses where these uncorrelated strategies fit in portfolios, current market opportunities, and conditions in the credit cycle including private credit.

item.title: Japan’s Strategic Awakening
item.dc:creator: Goldman Sachs
item.description: Goldman Sachs · GS Insights (web) — Japan faces a strategic inflection point driven by geopolitical shifts and technological transformation, requiring reassessment of its economic and policy position. The nation is navigating changes in the global landscape that may reshape trade relationships, defense posture, and technological capabilities.

item.title: Must Read Research: Shifting Econ Outlook; Concentration Bubble Risk; Semis; Prediction Mkts
item.dc:creator: Bank of America
item.description: Bank of America · Global Research Unlocked — This research episode examines several key market dynamics including a more hawkish Federal Reserve outlook, equity market rotation despite continued concentration, shifting artificial intelligence demand trends, and emerging activity in prediction markets. The analysis highlights how structural changes beneath surface-level market movements are reshaping investment opportunities across multiple asset classes.

item.title: Signals & Noise: Updating the Outlook—Growth Up and Policy Tightening Ahead
item.dc:creator: Bank of America
item.description: Bank of America · Global Research Unlocked — Bank of America's midyear economic update revises global growth upward while cutting inflation forecasts, yet maintains an expectation of persistent policy tightness with a more hawkish Federal Reserve than consensus anticipates. Key drivers include robust labor markets, sticky core inflation, geopolitical impacts on energy, and AI-driven export strength in Asia supporting overall growth. The U.S. economy continues to outperform other developed markets despite these cross-currents.

item.title: Comeback for Europe’s Bull Market?
item.dc:creator: Morgan Stanley
item.description: Morgan Stanley · Thoughts on the Market — European equities are experiencing a notable rally with year-to-date performance now matching the S&P 500's 7-8% gain, marking a recovery from earlier conflict-related lows. Morgan Stanley strategists discuss the potential for further broadening of this market rally and assess the implications of geopolitical developments including potential U.S.-Iran negotiations.

item.title: Increasing Transparency and Tradability in Private Credit
item.dc:creator: Apollo
item.description: Apollo · Apollo Insights & Outlook (web) — Apollo discusses how major credit markets evolve toward transparency and liquidity, noting that private credit is undergoing the same historical transformation from illiquid, opaque origination-and-hold models toward more frequent pricing and active trading. The firm argues this increased tradability parallels the maturation processes seen in other credit markets and helps establish scale within the asset class.
